notes: Specs sourced from the official Sony BRC-X400 Operating Instructions manual (ManualsLib), cross-referenced with the Lee Hartman & Sons AV catalog (av-iq.com). The MIC ports are labeled as a pair (×2) in the spec table; they appear as individual 3.5mm mini-jacks on the rear panel. The VISCA RS-422 terminals are labeled IN and OUT separately, each on an RJ-45 jack (total ×2 RJ-45 for RS-422). The EXT SYNC IN is present on BRC-X400 and BRC-X401 but absent on SRG-X400/X402/X120 variants. Power connector is the JEITA RC-5320A TYPE4 dc-barrel (closest match in controlled list); not a standard iec inlet.

ports.name: HDMI OUT
ports.note: HDMI Type A × 1; 4K output available via HDMI (and IP); version 1.4b compatible

ports.name: SDI OUT
ports.note: BNC × 1; 3G-SDI; HD output only (4K not supported via SDI)

ports.name: EXT SYNC IN
ports.note: BNC × 1; 75Ω; accepts HD 3-level sync or SD Black Burst; BRC-X400/X401 models only, not on SRG variants

ports.name: MIC 2
ports.note: Mini jack ø3.5mm; Mic/Line selectable; Y-adapter connection supported

ports.name: VISCA RS-422 IN
ports.note: RJ-45; RS-422 serial VISCA control; connect from remote controller or previous camera in daisy chain

ports.name: VISCA RS-422 OUT
ports.note: RJ-45; RS-422 serial VISCA control; connect to next camera in daisy chain; max cable length 1.2 km

ports.name: LAN
ports.note: RJ-45; 10/100/1000BASE-T; PoE+ (IEEE802.3at) power input; VISCA over IP, S700PTP, CGI control, and IP video streaming

ports.name: DC IN 12V
ports.note: IEC60130-10 (JEITA RC-5320A TYPE4); 5.5mm OD / 3.3mm ID / 1.0mm pin; DC 10.8–13.2V; supplied AC adapter (100–240V, 50/60Hz). Camera can also be powered via PoE+ on the LAN port instead.
